aliases: resolve a query into topic context
blurb: How a query becomes approved topic context: the matcher, keyword scoring, the route envelope, the --wiki output, and the topic-router skill.
intent: Understand how a freeform query resolves to approved topic context. Covers the priority-ordered matcher in lib/topics/route.py (alias/label exact -> ref-path exact -> ref-path substring -> label/intent substring, then a fuzzy multi-keyword fallback), the keyword-signal model (a wordfreq English-rarity prior in route.py scaled by the repo-adaptive query-log document-frequency multiplier in lib/topics/term_weights.py, plus a per-graph saturation filter that drops corpus-common terms), the route_topic envelope that returns ordered refs + bounded wiki pages + first-degree related topics with an approved/unmatched status, the best_topic_for_text reverse signal for long text, the generated topic-router skill, and the regin topics route/route --wiki/router-skill/rebuild-query-df CLI commands.
label: Topic routing (keyword router over the approved graph + topic-router skill)
